meta.title: PSLE Maths: Model Method, Problem Sums & MOE Syllabus
meta.description: Live online PSLE maths on the MOE syllabus: model method taught as thinking, problem-sum families, no-calculator Paper 1 craft, Singapore evening slots.
meta.level: Primary 4-6 students on the MOE syllabus, PSLE-bound
meta.commitment: 2 live one-hour classes per week + short practice sets
meta.certification: Course completion certificate + documented paper-score progression

program_overview.title: PSLE Maths Mastery
program_overview.tagline: Problem sums are a teachable skill. We teach it, 1-on-1, without the centre queue.
program_overview.description: A 12-month live program aimed squarely at PSLE mathematics, taught on the MOE syllabus at Singapore-friendly evening hours. The PSLE separates thinkers from drillers: multi-step problem sums, a no-calculator Paper 1, and AL scoring where consistency is everything. This course teaches the model method as a thinking tool rather than a template, names and trains the heuristics explicitly, builds calculator-free fluency in every class, and closes with paper craft, time discipline, working presentation and the mark-banking habits that protect AL bands.
what_makes_this_different: The model method taught as reasoning, part-whole, comparison, before-after and re-cutting, not as a ritual the school demands
Problem sums taught by structure family, so new problems look like old friends in disguise
MOE heuristics named and trained explicitly: work backwards, guess-and-check with discipline, restate, spot patterns
Paper 1 fluency drills open every class, because AL bands are lost one careless mark at a time
1-on-1 attention no centre batch of fifteen can give, at Singapore evening hours
Understanding-first: a child who can draw what the numbers are doing owns the problem
One dedicated mentor tracking exactly where your child's reasoning thins
Honest AL guidance from real timed-paper evidence, never invented promises

learning_path.phase_1: The method rebuilt properly: number fluency, model drawing as thinking, and the first problem-sum families
learning_path.phase_2: The P5-P6 canon: fractions of remainder, ratio, percentage, speed and geometry, family by family
learning_path.phase_3: Exam craft: Paper 1 and 2 technique, timed papers, mark-banking discipline and the calm P6 runway to the PSLE

career_outcomes: An AL band that reflects your child's real ability, not their exam nerves
Problem-sum confidence that carries into secondary E and A Maths
Calculator-free fluency that keeps paying long after the PSLE
A child who reads a hard problem and starts drawing instead of freezing

phase_1_foundation.title: PHASE 1: The Method, Rebuilt Properly (Months 1-4)
phase_1_foundation.description: The gap behind most PSLE struggles is a model method learned as template instead of thinking. This phase rebuilds number fluency and model drawing from meaning, then applies both to the first problem-sum families.

week_1.title: Diagnostic and the Honest AL Baseline
week_1.description: Full diagnostic: model-method depth, Paper 1 fluency, and exactly where problem sums break, with an honest AL baseline
topics: Model-method depth check across part-whole and comparison bars
Locating exactly where problem sums break down
Honest Achievement Level (AL) baseline from timed questions
Whole-number, fraction and decimal accuracy audit
week_1.practice: A short mixed diagnostic of Paper 1 items and two problem sums, marked to expose the real starting stage.
week_1.assessment: Baseline AL estimate plus a written map of which problem-sum families and Paper 1 skills need the most work.

week_2.title: Whole-Number Operations at Paper 1 Tempo
week_2.description: Whole-number operations at Paper 1 tempo; estimation as a checking habit
topics: Addition, subtraction, multiplication and division fluency
Long multiplication and long division without a calculator
Order of operations in multi-step calculations
Estimation and rounding as a checking habit
Mental strategies for speed on Paper 1 items
Place value and number sense to catch slips
week_2.practice: A timed whole-number drill set plus five estimation checks on given answers.

week_3.title: Fractions as Numbers, From Pictures First
week_3.description: Fractions as numbers: equivalence, comparison and operations from pictures first
topics: Fractions on a number line as genuine quantities
Multiplying and dividing fractions with meaning
Bar and area pictures behind every rule
week_3.practice: A fractions set moving from picture-drawn answers to fluent written computation.

week_4.title: Decimals and the Fraction-Decimal-Percentage Bridge
week_4.description: Decimals and the fraction-decimal-percentage bridge, to fluency
topics: Decimal place value and the four operations
Converting fractions to decimals and back
Fluency drills across all three forms
week_4.practice: Conversion drills and mixed decimal calculations at Paper 1 pace.

week_5_6.title: Part-Whole Models as Reasoning
week_5_6.description: Part-whole models: the child draws, the mentor questions, never the reverse
topics: Part-whole bar model as a picture of the numbers
Translating word phrases into bar segments
The child draws first, the mentor questions
Checking the model against the story
week_5_6.practice: Six part-whole problem sums where the bar is drawn before any calculation.

week_7_8.title: Comparison Models Made Visible
week_7_8.description: Comparison models: more-than, less-than and times-as-many, made visible
topics: Comparison bars for more-than and less-than
Aligning bars to reveal the difference
week_7_8.practice: A comparison-model set mixing additive and multiplicative relationships.

week_9_10.title: Unit Thinking and Re-Cutting Bars
week_9_10.description: Unit thinking: when to cut a bar into parts, and the re-cutting flexibility templates cannot supply
topics: Treating one unit as the engine of a model
Deciding how many parts to cut a bar into
Re-cutting a bar when the story changes
Finding unit value then scaling up
week_9_10.practice: Problems requiring the student to choose the cut, then re-cut when stuck.

week_11_12.title: First Structure Families: Sum-and-Difference
week_11_12.description: First structure families: sum-and-difference and equal-stage problems, solved by model and defended aloud
topics: Recognising the skeleton under the wording
Solving then explaining the reasoning aloud
Extending a solved problem to a variant
week_11_12.practice: A family-sorted set of sum-and-difference and equal-stage problem sums.

week_13.title: Work Backwards Heuristic
week_13.description: Work backwards: end-state problems unwound step by step
topics: Undoing add, subtract, multiply and divide
week_13.practice: A set of end-state problems solved by unwinding step by step.

week_14.title: Disciplined Guess-and-Check
week_14.description: Guess-and-check with discipline: the organised table, not wild stabs
topics: Setting up an organised guess-and-check table
Using each result to steer the next guess
Column headings that show the reasoning
Presenting the table for method marks
week_14.practice: Guess-and-check problems recorded in a clean, columned table.

week_15.title: Restate and Simplify
week_15.description: Restate and simplify: turning wordy problems into drawable ones
topics: Stripping a wordy problem to its core
Restating in the student's own words
Identifying the quantity actually asked for
Turning sentences into a drawable model
week_15.practice: Wordy problems rewritten in one line, then drawn and solved.

week_16.title: Phase 1 Checkpoint
week_16.description: Phase checkpoint: mixed problem-sum set solved, explained and extended
topics: Mixed problem-sum set across all Phase 1 families
Error log started from checkpoint mistakes
week_16.practice: A mixed timed problem-sum set covering every Phase 1 family.
week_16.assessment: Phase 1 checkpoint marked for method and reasoning, feeding the first error-log entries.

phase_2_problem_sums.title: PHASE 2: The P5-P6 Canon, Family by Family (Months 5-9)
phase_2_problem_sums.description: The problem-sum territory where the PSLE decides its top bands, taught by structure family so your child recognises the skeleton under every costume: fractions of remainder, ratio and its changes, percentage, speed and geometry.

month_5.title: Month 5: Fractions of Remainder and Branch Models

week_17_18.title: Remainder Concept and Branch Models
week_17_18.description: The remainder concept made visible: branch models and the re-cut into common parts
topics: Fraction of a remainder made visible
Tracking what is left after each stage
Restoring the whole from a known part
week_17_18.practice: Branch-model problems working from a remainder back to the whole.

week_19_20.title: Multi-Stage Remainder Problems
week_19_20.description: Classic remainder problems at PSLE standard, including the multi-stage versions that humble adults
topics: Combining branch models with unit value
week_19_20.practice: A set of exam-standard remainder problems, including multi-stage versions.

month_6.title: Month 6: Ratio, and Ratio That Changes

week_21_22.title: Ratio as Units
week_21_22.description: Ratio as units: equivalent ratios, sharing and the unit-value engine
topics: Sharing a quantity in a given ratio
The unit-value engine for ratio problems
week_21_22.practice: Ratio problems solved through the unit-value method with bar support.

week_23_24.title: Before-and-After Ratio Problems
week_23_24.description: Before-and-after problems: changed ratios, constant totals and constant differences, the PSLE's signature family
topics: Choosing the invariant to anchor units
week_23_24.practice: Before-and-after ratio sets sorted by which quantity stays constant.

week_25_26.title: Percentage Of, Increase and Decrease
week_25_26.description: Percentage of, increase and decrease, and GST-and-discount chains
topics: Finding a percentage of a quantity

week_27_28.title: Reverse Percentage at Exam Standard
week_27_28.description: Reverse percentage and percentage-with-model problems at exam standard
week_27_28.practice: Reverse-percentage and percentage-with-model problems at PSLE level.

month_8.title: Month 8: Speed, Rate and the Journey Problems

week_29_30.title: Speed as a Rate With Meaning
week_29_30.description: Speed as a rate with meaning: distance-time reasoning before formulas
topics: Speed understood as distance per unit time
Deriving the speed, distance, time triangle
Units and conversions in rate problems
Reading a journey from a description
week_29_30.practice: Distance-time problems solved by reasoning, then confirmed by formula.

week_31_32.title: Journey Problems and Average Speed
week_31_32.description: Meeting, catching-up and average-speed problems, drawn on journey diagrams
week_31_32.practice: Meeting, catching-up and average-speed problems on drawn journey diagrams.

month_9.title: Month 9: Geometry, Area and the Visual Problems

week_33_34.title: Angles and Figures With Justification
week_33_34.description: Angles, triangles and quadrilaterals with justification language; composite figures
topics: Angle facts for lines and triangles
Justifying each step with a reason
week_33_34.practice: Angle problems requiring a stated reason for every step.

week_35_36.title: Area, Perimeter and Shaded Regions
week_35_36.description: Area and perimeter problem sums including the shaded-region classics; circles at P6 standard
topics: Area and perimeter of composite shapes
Circle area and circumference at P6 standard
Fractions of a circle and semicircles
week_35_36.practice: Shaded-region and circle problem sums at PSLE standard.

phase_3_exam_craft.title: PHASE 3: Paper Craft and the PSLE Runway (Months 10-12)
phase_3_exam_craft.description: Understanding becomes AL bands here: Paper 1 speed without a calculator, Paper 2 working discipline, timed full papers with review rituals, and a peak plan that walks into the PSLE calm.

month_10.title: Month 10: Paper 1 and Paper 2 as Different Skills

week_37_38.title: Paper 1 Craft and Mental Strategies
week_37_38.description: Paper 1 craft: mental strategies, no-calculator discipline and the one-mark questions banked fast
topics: Banking one-mark and two-mark questions fast
week_37_38.practice: A full Paper 1 section timed, focused on banking the short questions.

week_39_40.title: Paper 2 Craft and Method Marks
week_39_40.description: Paper 2 craft: presentation of working, method marks, and calculator use that helps instead of wastes time
topics: Presentation of working for method marks
Showing enough working to secure marks
week_39_40.practice: Paper 2 questions written up to full method-mark presentation standard.

month_11.title: Month 11: Timed Papers and Mark Banking

week_41_42.title: Full Timed Papers and Mark Banking
week_41_42.description: Full timed papers at exam pacing; the banking discipline, secure marks first, wrestle the monsters last
topics: Full papers at true exam pacing
Leaving the hardest problems for last
Time budgeting across Paper 1 and Paper 2
Flagging and returning to hard items
week_41_42.practice: A complete timed paper sat under exam conditions with a pacing plan.

week_43_44.title: Review Rituals and the Error Log
week_43_44.description: Review rituals: why each mark was lost, and the named fix; error-log closure begins
topics: Reviewing why each mark was lost
Naming the specific fix for each error
Sorting errors by type and frequency
week_43_44.practice: Each lost mark logged with a named fix and a follow-up practice item.

week_45_48.title: Weakest-Family Rotations
week_45_48.description: Weakest-family rotations by error-log priority; confidence work through problems your child can nearly do, then can
topics: Priority set by the error log
Building momentum into the final weeks
week_45_48.practice: Rotating problem sets aimed at the top error-log priorities each week.

week_49_52.title: Final Cycles and the Peak-Week Plan
week_49_52.description: Final paper cycles with documented progression; peak-week plan, light practice, rest, and the exam met as a rehearsed place
topics: Rest and routine before the exam
The PSLE met as a rehearsed place
week_49_52.practice: Final timed papers with progression recorded, tapering into light peak-week practice.
week_49_52.assessment: Final timed PSLE-format papers scored and charted to confirm AL-trend progression before the exam.

faqs.question: Is this course aligned to the MOE syllabus and the actual PSLE format?
faqs.answer: Yes, the MOE primary mathematics syllabus is the spine, and exam work uses the real format: the no-calculator Paper 1, Paper 2 with calculator, and AL scoring awareness throughout. We verify current SEAB formats for your child's cohort rather than assuming.

faqs.question: How do the class timings work from Singapore?
faqs.answer: Comfortably, Singapore evenings after school and CCA map to our mentors' prime teaching hours, so 6 to 9 pm SGT slots are genuinely available, as are weekend mornings. You pick two weekly slots and they stay yours.

faqs.question: My child computes well but freezes on problem sums. Can this be fixed?
faqs.answer: Yes, problem sums are the most teachable 'talent' in Singapore maths. They break into structure families, each with a model shape and heuristic. We teach the families, then the re-cutting flexibility that separates thinkers from template-followers. A term of that work changes what your child sees when they read a problem.

faqs.question: My child already attends a tuition centre. Why switch?
faqs.answer: Sometimes you should not, a child thriving near the top of a good batch should stay. The switch wins when the batch pace is wrong in either direction: the struggling child whose specific gaps never get airtime, or the capable child rehearsing what they already know. Here, every minute of the hour aims at your child.

faqs.question: When should PSLE prep start?
faqs.answer: The full 12-month arc suits P5, finishing on the P6 runway; P6 students join a compressed version prioritised by the diagnostic. Earlier starts (P4) spend longer in the method phase, which is the highest-return work there is.

faqs.question: What AL improvement can we expect?
faqs.answer: We will not invent a number. What you get: an honest baseline in week one, the highest-value families attacked first, and timed-paper evidence you can track yourself. AL bands reward consistency, and consistency is a trainable behaviour.

faqs.question: What does the course cost?
faqs.answer: Small-group classes are ₹1,499 per month and private 1-on-1 mentorship is ₹4,999 per month in India; international students, including Singapore families, pay USD $100 per month for group and $150 for 1-on-1, with 8 live one-hour classes a month and recordings included. Singapore centres typically charge SGD 250-500 monthly for batches of 8-15.

faqs.question: Can we try it before paying?
faqs.answer: Yes. Every child starts with a free live demo class at a Singapore evening hour that doubles as the diagnostic, no card details, no obligation.

testimonials.text: My child Dhairya is really enjoying the classes. This is his first online class, and he eagerly looks forward to it. I can see his improvement.

course_guarantees.live_classes: Live, interactive classes with a real instructor, never pre-recorded videos.
course_guarantees.small_batches: Small batches only: group classes are capped at 10 students, with mini-batch (3 to 4 students) and personal 1-on-1 options.
course_guarantees.structured_curriculum: A structured, well-paced curriculum taught step by step, with hands-on practice in every session.
course_guarantees.doubt_support: Doubt support between classes over WhatsApp, so you are never left stuck.
course_guarantees.certificate: A course-completion certificate you can share.
course_guarantees.free_demo: A free demo class before you enrol, so you can decide with no pressure.
